title: Procedures for Suspension of the Accreditation of Principal/Employer
content: 43 or Deactivation of Vessel Enrollment. Upon receipt of an advice, complaint, or report 44 of an alleged violation, the Department may issue an Order of Suspension of the 45 Accreditation of the Principal/Employer or an Order of Deactivation of the Vessel 46 Enrollment based on initial evaluation if there exists substantial ground/s for suspension 47 or deactivation. The Order shall have the effect of suspending the documentary 48 processing of the principal/employer or deactivating the vessel enrollment at the 49 Department. 50 51 The Department shall require the principal/employer to submit their Comment within a 52 period of five (5) calendar days from receipt of the Order of Suspension or Deactivation. 53 After evaluation, the suspension of accreditation or deactivation of vessel enrollment may 54 be lifted upon satisfactory settlement of the seafarers’ claim/s or compliance with the 55 conditions for lifting the suspension or deactivation. Otherwise, a recommendation for 56 disqualification will be made for the principal/employer from participating in the overseas 57 employment program. 58 59
